steps.title: Apply the y-intercept definition
steps.content:
The y-intercept occurs at $x = 0$. Substituting into $y = 16^{x+3}$:
$$y = 16^{0+3} = 16^3$$

steps.title: Evaluate $16^3$
steps.content: $16^3 = 16 \times 16 \times 16 = 256 \times 16 = 4096$. The y-intercept is $(0, 4096)$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
